properties.name: Rathi Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: Rajasthan's best dual-purpose milk cattle — the 'Kamdhenu of Rajasthan'. Homes tract: Bikaner, Ganganagar, Hanumangarh.
Named after the Rath (nomadic Muslim pastoralist) community; average lactation 1,500–2,000 kg over 300 days.
The Rathi breeding centre at Nokha (Bikaner) is India's flagship Rathi genetic-improvement facility.

properties.name: Tharparkar Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: The world's best desert dairy breed — pale grey coat, hump; thrives on scanty desert forage.
Home tract straddles the Thar Parkar region (Sindh) and India's Barmer-Jaisalmer border.
Chotan Cattle Breeding Farm (Barmer) is Rajasthan's principal Tharparkar conservation herd; lactation 1,000–1,500 kg.

properties.name: Nagauri Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: Rajasthan's premier draught cattle — long horns, powerful build; historically pulled the Marwar army's cannon wagons.
Named after Nagaur district; the bullocks are prized for tilling heavy Nagaur loam.
Poor milk yield (~500 kg/lactation) — a specialist plough-and-cart breed, not a dairy breed.

properties.name: Kankrej Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: India's largest cattle breed — bulls weigh 500-600 kg with distinctive lyre-shaped horns.
Home tract straddles the Rajasthan-Gujarat border (Kankrej taluka, Banaskantha); Rajasthan population concentrated in Barmer-Jalore.
Dual-purpose (milk + draught); the American Brahman breed was developed by crossing Kankrej with three other Indian breeds.

properties.name: Malvi Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: Draught breed of the Malwa plateau — white/grey coat, medium build; excellent for the heavy black soils of Jhalawar-Baran.
Named after the Malwa region that spans MP and southeastern Rajasthan (Hadoti).
Bullocks are prized for cart-pulling on the plateau's rough terrain; the cows give ~600 kg milk/lactation.

properties.name: Mewati (Hariana) Cattle
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: Sturdy draught breed of the Matsya region — reddish-brown coat; a cousin of the neighbouring Hariana breed of Haryana.
Home tract in Alwar-Bharatpur-Dholpur; used both for milk (moderate) and heavy field work.
Numbers falling with mechanisation of Bharatpur's mustard fields — now a NBAGR conservation-priority breed.

properties.name: Chokla Sheep
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: India's finest carpet-wool sheep — white fleece, small size, thrives in the Shekhawati semi-arid tract.
Fleece is exported for Persian and Kashmiri carpet weaving; average wool yield 1.5 kg/year.
Bikaner's Central Sheep and Wool Research Institute (CSWRI) branch runs the Chokla nucleus flock.

properties.name: Marwari Sheep
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: The most numerous sheep breed of Rajasthan — black face, medium build; a hardy Marwar-Thar dual-purpose (wool + mutton) breed.
Nomadic Raika shepherds herd Marwari flocks across the Barmer-Jodhpur-Jaisalmer belt during monsoon migrations.
Coarse carpet wool; ~1 kg/animal/year; the mutton is prized for its low fat content.

properties.name: Sirohi Goat
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + NBAGR breed registry

facts: India's most-farmed meat goat — reddish-brown coat with light spots, dual-purpose (milk + meat).
Rapidly gains weight (25-30 kg by 12 months); popular for stall-feeding across North India.
Home tract in Sirohi's Aravalli foothills; ~4 million goats of this breed across Rajasthan.
Rajasthan's principal draught camel — dark brown, heavy build; served as the mount of the Ganga Risala (Bikaner Camel Corps).
Home tract in Bikaner, Ganganagar, Churu; the National Research Centre on Camel at Bikaner is India's flagship camel institute.
Rajasthan holds ~85% of India's camels; population has fallen from 500k (2007) to under 250k (2019) — a conservation crisis.
Rajasthan's fastest camel breed — light-tan coat, tall and lean; the preferred riding camel of the Bhati Rajputs.
The Jaisalmer Desert Festival's camel race is contested exclusively on this breed.
Home tract in deep Thar (Jaisalmer, Barmer); requires less water than Bikaneri camels but slower to mature.
Rajasthan's rarest and shortest camel breed — home tract in the Aravalli foothills of Mewar (Rajsamand, Udaipur, Chittor).
Used historically by the Sisodias as a pack animal in the hilly Mewar terrain — Bikaneri camels cannot manage the slopes.
Population under 10,000; NRC-Camel Bikaner runs a Mewari conservation herd at Sadri.
